content.verdict: Amankora Bumthang sits in the spiritual heartland of Bhutan, in a valley dense with temples, monasteries, and sacred lakes. Built in dzong-style with traditional rammed-earth walls and cantilevered timber roofs, the 16-suite lodge is the deepest cultural immersion of any Aman in the country — ideal for honeymooners who want a meaningful, contemplative stretch of their journey.
content.best_room: All 16 suites are identical with a bukhari fireplace, soaking tub, and Bhutanese textile detailing throughout. Request an upper-floor suite for the most privacy and the best views over the dzong-style courtyard.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Bumthang
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly Bhutan's small domestic flight from Paro to Bumthang (40 minutes), or drive 9-10 hours overland. Welcome butter tea, in-suite hot stone bath, fireside dinner at the dzong-style dining hall.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Jakar Dzong and Jambay Lhakhang
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Jakar Dzong, the 'castle of the white bird,' overlooking the valley. Afternoon at Jambay Lhakhang, one of the 108 temples built by King Songtsen Gampo in 659 AD — among the oldest in Bhutan.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Burning Lake and Tamshing Lhakhang
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit the Mebar Tsho ('burning lake'), a sacred pilgrimage site, then Tamshing Lhakhang, founded by Pema Lingpa in 1501 with original 500-year-old murals. Picnic lunch by the river.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Textile workshop
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the day with a master weaver learning the basics of yathra — Bumthang's distinctive woolen cloth. Choose your own pattern; the lodge can ship a finished piece home. Couples massage in the late afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kurjey Lhakhang and Tang valley
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Kurjey Lhakhang where Guru Rinpoche meditated in a cave, leaving a body imprint. Afternoon drive into the remote Tang valley for a farmhouse lunch and a hike to Ogyen Choling Manor.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa and stillness
itinerary_7_nights.description: Yoga at sunrise, leisurely breakfast, hot stone bath. Afternoon hike to a hilltop chorten with prayer flags. Romantic candlelit dinner served fireside in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the courtyard terrace, fly or drive back to Paro for the international Druk Air departure.

caveats: Bumthang is the most remote of the Amankora lodges — fly the domestic Bhutan Airlines hop from Paro to save 9-10 hours of driving.
The valley sits at 2,800m — pace your first day to acclimatize.
Sustainable Development Fee of $200 per person per night applies.
Mid-winter (Dec-Feb) can drop well below freezing at night — pack layers and expect bukhari fires running constantly.

faqs.question: Is Amankora Bumthang good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — quieter and more spiritually focused than the other Amankora lodges. Couples who want temple visits, weaving, and quiet evenings by the fire will love it.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: September-November and March-May. October's Jambay Lhakhang Drup festival, with its naked fire dance, is one of Bhutan's most sacred festivals.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.answer: Four to six months. Combining Bumthang with two or three other Amankora lodges is the standard itinerary and books up early.

faqs.question: Do we need a guide?
faqs.answer: Yes — required by Bhutanese law and included in the Aman package.

faqs.question: What's the best room type?
faqs.answer: All 16 suites are identical. Request an upper-floor suite for views and quiet.

faqs.question: How do we get to Amankora Bumthang?
faqs.answer: Fly into Paro, then take the 40-minute Bhutan Airlines domestic hop to Bumthang. Aman handles all logistics.
